name: Spaghetti with Swordfish and Hot Peppers
recipeIngredient: 1 pound fresh swordfish, skin removed, cut into 1/4 inch cubes
recipeInstructions:
1. Season swordfish with sea salt and freshly ground black pepper.
2. Bring 6 quarts water to boil and add 2 tablespoons salt.
3. In a 12 to 14-inch saute pan, heat olive oil, garlic, and jalapenos over medium heat until softened but not browned, about 5 to 6 minutes.
4. Add tomatoes, white wine, and swordfish to the pan; simmer for 5 to 6 minutes, or until the swordfish is just cooked through.
5. Stir in mint leaves and mix well with the swordfish mixture.
6. Remove from heat. Cook pasta according to package instructions until just al dente, then drain.
7. Pour hot pasta into the pan with the swordfish; stir to combine.
8. Serve hot, sprinkled with fennel leaves.
Yield: 4 servings
